title: Adding Meta Pixel To Snyder Site
description:
Seeking help add the metapixel from the client please.
https://snyderac.com/

Here are the script and steps they provided:
 
Install Meta Pixel on your website
The Meta Pixel is a piece of code that you add to your website by copying the base code below and pasting it into the header section of your website.


1. Copy base code
Copy the Meta Pixel base code below.
<!-- Meta Pixel Code -->
<script>
!function(f,b,e,v,n,t,s)
{if(f.fbq)return;n=f.fbq=function(){n.callMethod?
n.callMethod.apply(n,arguments):n.queue.push(arguments)};
if(!f._fbq)f._fbq=n;n.push=n;n.loaded=!0;n.version='2.0';
n.queue=[];t=b.createElement(e);t.async=!0;
t.src=v;s=b.getElementsByTagName(e)[0];
s.parentNode.insertBefore(t,s)}(window, document,'script',
'https://connect.facebook.net/en_US/fbevents.js');
fbq('init', '1602864097646186');
fbq('track', 'PageView');
</script>
<noscript><img height="1" width="1" style="display:none"
src="https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=1602864097646186&ev=PageView&noscript=1"
/></noscript>
<!-- End Meta Pixel Code -->

2. Paste base code to website
Paste the Meta Pixel code between the <head> and </head> tags of your web page. You may already have other existing code between the head tags, so just place the pixel code underneath that, but above </head>. Install the base code on every page of your website. See more detailed instructions

3. Save website code changes
Before returning to this screen, refresh your website to save and confirm the changes you made to your website's code.

 ----
Thanks much!

title: Update schedule script on site
description:
Hi, this is inline w/ the discussion: Update Scheduler  - J301 SnyderAC

Client would like to update their scheduler script on the site. For their Book Now button.
https://snyderac.com/

As per client: they created an updated scheduler to be used on the Snyder to go on the ‘Book Now’ buttons. This scheduler is location agnostic, meaning that it does not need to be different based on the location selector. Once a user puts their zip code into the scheduler, it will direct them to the appropriate North/South Tenant. 

The script information is below, the old script needs to be replaced or removed for the new to work:
 
<script
  data-router-id="router_buljbmtiohzg7ry3tei0wmk8"
  defer
  id="se-widget-embed"
  src="https://embed.scheduler.servicetitan.com/scheduler-v1.js"
></script>
 
<button
  onclick="_scheduler.show()"
  type="button">
  Book Now!
</button>
 
 
Linked below is a test link to show you what the new scheduler should look like to ensure it is created correctly.
https://scheduler.servicetitan.com/router/router_buljbmtiohzg7ry3tei0wmk8?inlineMode=true

title: Website change requests...
assignees: Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, Keith Bruss
description:
Quite considerable changes to the website requested here from Snyder. Let's all four of us get on a call to discuss this. I can deal with the SoW request in part 2 of the email...

-------------

Hi, Chris and team; I hope you are all doing well.

As we discussed last time when we discussed website changes and how to improve the Snyder experience for customers in the North and South, and as you requested, here is the list of changes/action items we need to scope out to achieve what we consider would get us closer to an ideal customer experience stage.

Some of these items have higher priority, so that is something we can discuss once you confirm this is all possible and we get a SOW.

In the spirit of transparency, please let us know if these changes are feasible and, with the scope, provide us with a timeline of execution.

Last but not least, we would like some more clarity on the current scope of work and more details on the amount of content we can produce on a monthly basis, this can be in work hours*, words, pages*, deliverables, etc - but something that details what can we request/plan each month with you. *If it's hours, please specify what kind of deliverables can be achieved on average within a working hour, if it's pages, what the specifications of such pages are, etc.

Please let me know if you have any questions or require any clarification! Hopefully, we can have a scope of work quoted that we can review in our next call.

Thanks in advance and have a great weekend!

Best,
Elvis Quintero El Yordi
Marketing Manager – South Florida
941-299-2559 - elvis.yordi@teamsoutheast.com

ATTACHMENT...

 
 
UI/UX:

  • Ensure Auto IP-Detection works as intended, when user visits they are presented with a pop-up to validate they are on the Snyder south/north region and they are prompted to change with two images side to side with the North and South maps with the corresponding counties for each shaded in the Snyder blue. (Considerable amount of work and definitely out of scope)

  • On the main categories (electrical, drains, cooling, etc.) Add tiles for the subcategories right after the hero image for easier navigation. 
    • When navigating electrical, the tiles would be the electrical sub-categories. When navigating plumbing, the tiles would be plumbing sub-categories, etc. (Not entirely sure what they mean here. Tiles on the actual pages of subcategories after the header image?)

  • Main Navigation: 
    • If browsing in the South region, remove "Heating" and "Generators" from Mega Menu. These are not relevant for the market. (Different menus per location? A lot of work and out of scope. Possible not even desirable from an SEO point of view.)
    • Combine Cooling & Heating to clean up redundancy and improve UX for the North region. (I don't think this is a good idea. Discuss)
    • Remove Contact subcategories from Mega Menu. Leave it as just a button that leads to Contact Us. (Not normally done since users look in the menu for the contact page but worthy of discussion)
       
      • Add a basic Contact Form with captcha. (New direction for Snyder. (This will need to be approved by Shawn I think)
    •  
      • Remove the rest of the subcategories since its redundant with About Us (i.e., Careers) (I don't know what they're thinking about here. Not a good idea)
    •  
      • Move “24/7 Emergency Service” page to About. (Hmmmm, maybe duplicate the link to there. I don't think it's a good idea to just move it.)
    •  
  • Move up the “Where you’ll find Snyder” section in the home page – up. Right below offers. (Hmmm, not good for SEO)
  • The following pages should be dynamic to each region (north/south). By dynamic, we should be able to provide different content depending on the region as offers and some services will vary. (So they basically want two different sites)
     
    • Home Page (Hero Image, Different “Where you’ll find Snyder” section, and other content.
  •  
    • Offer page  

    • Maintenance Plan 

    • About Us/History (South needs context for the southeast added to it) 

    • Service Area
  •  
    • Our Service Reviews
  •  
    • Careers
  •  
    • Within each main category (cooling, electrical, plumbing, generators, etc.), offers should be “dynamic” or accurate depending on the region (north/south)
  •  
    • 24/7 Emergency Service
  •  
    • Counties and Cities 

  • Hero Images for the Home Page and main categories (Cooling/Cooling & Heating, Plumbing, Drains, Electrical, Generators, Contact, About) should have the possibility to be dynamic and change depending on the region (north/south).
  • Add "Buy Filters" button next to the Book Now button in the Hero Image (Home Page) (Do we have a shop on the site with filters?)

PPC Attribution & Tracking

·      PPC Pages should lead to its corresponding category/subcategory based on the campaign strategy.

o   Current state has PPC completely isolated in its own folders making us constantly lose campaign attribution. For example: https://snyderac.com/south-ppc/plumbing-services/water-heater-repair/
 
·      Phone numbers should dynamically stick throughout the user’s experience when they come from a paid channel, i.e., PPC. (WhatConverts can do this - we have spoken to them about it)
 
·      Contact Form should fire tags based on user source/medium, so we can track conversions accordingly. (I think we have code that can do this - but of course, no forms. Maybe he's talking about PPC forms, which we're not in control of?)

·      CapacityPRO Integration to dynamically change offers and copy on the website based on our operational capacity. (CapacityPRO appears to be a CampDigital brand)

o   More from partnering with Camp Digital on this. (Yes I think all of this section looks like something they should take up with CampDigital?)

Content:

  • Main Headers/Hero texts: Add shadow for readability. (Needs new font then)
  • Headers/Hero Text need improved spacing between letters and shadows for better readability.  (Needs new font then)
  • Hero images need to change and update more between the categories/sub categories, this creates a diverse journey that reinforces distinction between pages. (We've been through this with Shawn - they need to supply us with these images)
  • All content on the non-local pages to be Florida oriented, not Jacksonville or South oriented. 
    • Complete consistency across all main categories and subcategories with "Florida" w/o any distinction of locality. (SEO need to discuss this. We've been over it quite a few times with tis new team.)

  • All URL structures need to match Florida and not favor one location or the other. (SEO need to discuss this. We've been over it quite a few times with tis new team.)
  • Schemas, image tags, ALT tags, also need to be broad to Florida and not favor one location or the other. (SEO need to discuss this. We've been over it quite a few times with tis new team.)
  • Incorporate Snyderman throughout the site.
     
    • We can expand more on this item, but this means including the photos we have of him through the experience and design as it makes sense in the trade categories. We have lots of photos and content available. (We need all of these photos and instructions on exactly which pages to add them to)
  •  
  • About Service area and Local Pages:
     
    • Depending on the region being browsed, the county/city list should move up, Hero Image & Headers should be different and the region the user is browsing should be prioritized. (Complete change of direction)
  •  
    • Add video to About Us and Brand “Welcome” Pages. (Something we can do!)
       
      • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JG69rS8KBL8
    •  
      • Ex of Welcome Page: https://snyderac.com/welcome-air-temp/


    • Need to complete all cities to strengthen local SEO. (SEO question)
  •  
    • All local pages should have internal links leading to the main categories or subcategories as appropriate following internal linking best practices. (SEO question, possibly use breadcrumbs?)
  •  
    • Within the county local pages, we need a template for the team that serves that area, this can be a sub-page of the county pages for additional depth to Google. (Not quite sure what this means, maybe bring into this?)
  •  

  • Footer: 

  • Locations need to be clickable. (Can do!)
  • North Florida & South Florida should lead to their own Service Area Pages which are subpages of Service Area. (Again, two different sites)
  • Maintain GMAP miniature should match the location the user is browsing.  (Current state) (Maps swap - not already doing this?)

Blog:

·      What is the current scope for blog?

·      Add Author pages for each region so each blog entry as a “subject matter expert” and adds a face to these blogs  – Snyder to provide base biographies. (I suppose we could do this. SEO implications?)

·      Community Engagement news/articles should be dynamic depending on the user’s region. (Out of scope)

o   Hide north efforts in the south page and viceversa. (Out of scope)

·      Keyword-dense articles that help boost local presence of both regions (separate blog posts). (SEO question)

·      Snyderman “Tips” as a monthly series (2 per month). (Fine, if they supply us with content we can post it)

·      Overall we are thinking 1-2 blog entries per week. (not inclusive of Community Engagement) [Need to understand current scope] (SEO question)


Mobile 

  • Ensure the above changes are mobile friendly. (Of course)

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.

description:
Here's the brief...

We need a new silo for Generators on the site for all locations, North & South Florida. Therefore the current Home Generator Installation menu link will come out of the Electrical drop down and be placed under a new drop down called "Generators". will need to be involved probably to do that, at least to check it's going to work on mobile.

The pages have to match https://assurancepower.com/ for titles and content style. This content ranks really well in Florida for these items:



Grab this content and rewrite it enough to be unique and not really traceable back to this site. Debra and Brandt want to approve this content before launch. After that we need to optimize it to rank in organics, not maps, so we may need some other supporting pages or blogs etc. These generators cost upwards of $10k and we're going to have to consider ranking content at all points of the customer journey as they are not likely to just call Snyder after seeing the first maps ranking.

Also consider setting up a lead magnet with a form to capture leads for follow up later. Here is what Assurance Power use:



Maybe sign up to that to see what they are offering for the buying guide.

Here is Apex's guide to what kind of content they would like - It's quite extensive so please let me know if you have any questions:



It also occurred to me that the location switcher makes these main pages location agnostic so a bit of thought may be required to rank these for all North & South Florida locations. Again, let me know of any questions.

title: Please confirm we have all of the following for PhoenixAir...
description:
Apex have requested we move PhoenixAir to their wpEngine account, and Debra has asked us to confirm we have all of the following. , can you check the items on the list below please, and list out any we don't have?

---------------

  • Access:
    • Full site files
    • GA4
    • Google Search Console
    • GTM - single site 

  • Confirmed the following are ready:
    • any spam blocking
    • plugins are available in new environment 

  • That Clixsy is confident that no issues will exist post site move 

, I've highlighted the items in brown above to make a point about scope creep. We are not contracted to move this site over to wpEngine. This is an add on to their postponed plan to redirect pages from this site to Snyderac as part of the rebranding process.

Internally they have decided to put this back until after the summer, and just really assumed that we will do the extra non-priced work to move the site from their old agency servers to the Apex wpEngine one.

I think we are happy to do this if we are moving the site 'as-is' from the site files we've been given; however, all these checks have now crept in, which are really unreasonable. Part of it is because we issued a couple of warnings to them after the last move that there may be premium plugins etc that need new accounts taken out. Since we issued those warnings it seems to have 'become our job' to perform site audits to search for and presumably fix issues like this for this site, and any future sites we are required to move.

This is how tricky and insidious scope creep is - a service we performed as a courtesy and as a responsible agency, suddenly becomes an expected and unpaid part of our scope of work. I'm not saying we shouldn't necessarily do it but this needs to be highlighted and monitored, and pointed out to the client.

title: Snyder Website
description:
Hi, seeking help w/ Juliana's request below.
I need someone to help me with verifying the Snyder domain for Facebook. Facebook provided the following options for verification:


Add a meta-tag to your HTML source code
  • Copy the meta-tag you see on your Business settings screen.
  • Open your website’s home page HTML source.
  • Paste the meta-tag into the <head>....</head> section of the HTML code, and publish the page.
  • Go to the root domain of your website, and view the HTML source to confirm that the meta-tag is there.

Upload an HTML file to your root directory
  • Download the HTML verification file that’s linked on your Business settings screen. Remember, don’t make any changes to the file after you download it.
  • Upload the HTML file to the root directory of your domain.
  • Go back to Business settings, and click on the link in step 3 to confirm that you can see the verification code on your website.

Update the DNS TXT record with your domain registrar
  • Go to your domain registrar and log in.
  • Go to the DNS records section.
  • Go back to your Business settings screen and copy the TXT record in step 2.
  • Add the TXT record in your DNS configuration by following the instructions from your domain registrar.
  • Use a DNS TXT lookup tool to confirm the TXT record has been updated across your servers. 

, I already have the HTML file that Juliana sent.

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466
